schema_version: v0 — migrates when Q6 (intention envelope) lands
owner: akiendelllinux_cc_0 (CC) — CC gates writes; Akien-ratified (signature gate, 2026-07-23 design conversation: name is `intentions-model-compiler` (not `intentions-compiler`) — 'right now that folder is the model'; `intentions-compiler` deliberately RESERVED for a future front-end that ingests a page of prose into intentions; 'sorted.').
intention: The single owner-gated door that compiles the `intentions/` MODEL — regenerating `intentions/` as a pure projection from its sources (`intentions-other/` plus the charters homed beside the code), so that one folder can be carried to a bare machine and regrow the system (CLAUDE.md: `intentions/` is COMPILED from `intentions-other/` plus the charters). It is reached exactly ONE way (its compile door) but POKED from three: `cairn compile` from the CLI (via cairn-command), the `/sorted` resolution pivot, and a WRITE GATE that fires when a source changes (the live recompile-on-change path — the file-watching that was moved to GATES, event-driven, not a poller). The name says MODEL on purpose: right now the folder IS the model, so this compiles the model. `intentions-compiler` is reserved (unbuilt, no intention yet) for a possible future front-end that ingests a page of authored prose INTO intentions — a distinct concern the reserved name keeps free.
why: Law 4 + the roots table: a compiled view is never hand-edited, and `intentions/` is the carry-to-a-bare-machine model — so it must be REGENERABLE from its sources by physics, not kept current by whoever remembers to. That regeneration needs one write-door (Law 6 — exactly one owner gates writes to `intentions/`) and a proof a hollow build could not pass (Law 8 — a compile that silently drops or mangles a source is the defect). Poked-from-many-but-one-door is the shape: the CLI hand-crank, the /sorted pivot, and the write gate all POKE the same door, none of them writes `intentions/` themselves. Kin to the PROJECTOR (charter-state-history-split — state compiled from a component's history): same pattern (pure projection, single append/write door, never hand-edited), different corpus (per-component state vs the system-wide intentions model) and different owner — cross-referenced, NOT merged (asked the why of each: local-component-truth vs carry-to-bare-machine-model — not exclusive-and-terminal).
traces_to: Law 4 (a rule that matters is physics — `intentions/` is regenerable from sources, not hand-maintained; the 'compiled view is never hand-edited' IOU in CLAUDE.md 'Rules awaiting physics')
Law 6 (exactly one owner gates writes to `intentions/` — the compile door; every trigger pokes it, none writes directly)
Law 1 (the model is compiled once from structure, not re-derived by each reader — the same move as the projector, one corpus out)
Law 8 (the compile carries a proof a hollow build could not pass — a dropped/mangled source goes RED)
the roots table (CLAUDE.md: `intentions/` is COMPILED from `intentions-other/` + the beside-code charters — the folder carried to a bare machine to regrow the system)
kinship: charter-state-history-split (the projector — same projection pattern, different corpus/owner; cross-referenced not merged)
state: code-seam@v1: THINKME -> TICKETME -> BUILDME -> [PROVEME] -> LEARNME -> PROVED   (child a (the compile door) BUILT + PROVEN + SEALED + COMMITTED 2026-07-23, cairn 40d8306: cairn/intentions_model_compiler/ projects BOTH source trees into intentions/_model.json — 18 intentions, roots first by born timestamp; 7 teeth green under the tester, VALIDATION beside-code; charter at the component. The real model committed to CairnCommons f48b3c2. A MEASURED finding drove the born source — git --follow, after plain first-add sank the relocated roots. The `cairn compile` CLI face ALSO DONE (bin/cmd/compile, cairn 8db649a, wired end to end via cairn-command). Child c, the WRITE GATE, BUILT + PROVEN + WIRED 2026-07-23: a FileChanged host-seam — recompile_gate.sh (class-space/git, 3 teeth green under the tester, VALIDATION beside-code at validations/test_recompile_gate.json: it pokes the door, reverts a stale model, and is non-blocking-but-loud-in-the-record) plus the hook wiring in .claude/settings.local.json (instance-space, gitignored). Host-NOTICING not owner-EMITTING — an IOU standing in for the emit-chokepoint owner-gate emit (state-machine-physics.json) that is the resting state; Akien-ratified as the PRECEDENT template for mounting borrowed CC event-primitives (Routines, hooks) behind Cairn's own doors, cast from the 2026-07-23 sweep that measured the mid-2026 CC feature landscape against Cairn. FileChanged verified to fire on EXTERNAL writes (catches a human hand-edit, not only agent tool-writes); its live firing awaits an out-of-turn verify. REMAINING for child c: the intentions-other/ half of the watch (heterogeneous source names + unconfirmed cross-repo watch scope — filed in the charter host_seam edges). Plus a filed edge on gather — a repo-root bin/ charter is not yet gathered (gather scans cairn/*/). CORRECTED 2026-07-23: an earlier draft cast a bespoke 'repo-scanner event-fired on commit' here — WRONG twice over. The live recompile trigger is a GATE (Akien: the file-watching 'was moved to gates'), not a scanner; and the NIGHTLY repo sweep is the INSPECTOR regime (held-inspectors-janitors-filters), a separate already-held node — an inspector NEVER ACTS (it issues a finding, e.g. 'model stale vs sources'), and its JANITOR, not this compiler, would poke the door. So no scanner child lives here.)
gates: proof gate (tester) — a proof a hollow build could not pass: give the compiler a known set of sources (`intentions-other/` entries + beside-code charters) and assert the projected `intentions/` contains exactly what the sources imply — every source represented, nothing invented, a changed source changes its output, a REMOVED source removes its projection (no stale carry-over); assert `intentions/` is written ONLY through the compile door (a hand-edit is overwritten on next compile — the view is never authoritative); assert the compile is DETERMINISTIC (same sources -> same output, so drift is detectable). VALIDATION persisted beside-code via cairn/tester/validation_store.persist_validation and grep back. Durability gate: proven, persisted, committed (git).
falsifier: the compiler regenerates `intentions/` as a faithful, deterministic projection of its sources — every source represented, nothing invented, removals propagated, a hand-edit to `intentions/` reverted on next compile (the view is never a record of truth). Wrong-shape signals: (1) `intentions/` is written by any door OTHER than the compiler (a second writer — Law 6 breach); (2) the compile is non-deterministic or lossy (a source silently dropped — Law 8 defect, and drift becomes invisible); (3) the compiler starts AUTHORING content not in its sources (it has become an editor, not a projector — the compiled view has become a rival record of truth, Law 7); (4) it grows to also own the projector's job (per-component state) — the two corpora collapsed when they were meant to stay distinct vantages; (5) it grows its OWN nightly scanner (that work belongs to the inspector regime — a compiler that inspects has taken on a second job it does not own).
horizon: first real recompile-on-write — when a charter or `intentions-other/` source is written, a GATE fires, pokes this door, and `intentions/` regenerates without a human running anything; re-check then: is beside-code source-walking fast enough on every write, or does the gate need to hand the compiler a CHANGED-SET rather than trigger a full reprojection? And revisit the projector kinship — if the two compilers end up sharing real projection machinery, factor the shared core out (not before the second member proves the seam).
children: (buildable now, cairn) a — THE COMPILE DOOR: the pure projection `intentions-other/` + beside-code charters -> `intentions/`, as the single owner-gated write-door for `intentions/`. Deterministic, lossless, removal-propagating; a hand-edit to the compiled folder is reverted on next run. This is the engine every trigger pokes (and /sorted, and `cairn compile`). Berths in cairn/ beside the folder it owns; this ticket migrates there to become its history when it proves. NO blocking deps — build this first.

(the CLI face, waits on cairn-command) b — the `cairn compile` verb: a `bin/cmd/compile` that pokes the compile door from the command line — the manual hand-crank, one of the three triggers. A thin registration against the cairn-command dispatcher.

(BUILT + PROVEN + WIRED 2026-07-23, host-seam) c — the WRITE GATE: 'a source was written' fires a poke of the compile door — the LIVE recompile-on-change (shrinking-footprint — the write IS the event, no polling). Built as a FileChanged HOST-SEAM: recompile_gate.sh (git, proven — 3 teeth) + a hook in .claude/settings.local.json (instance-space). Host-NOTICING, an IOU standing in for the owner-gate emit (the emit-chokepoint physics state-machine-physics.json tracks) that is the resting state — kin to cairn-command's PATH seam (a host dependency named as debt, not faked into physics), retires when that emit lands. Covers the beside-code charters; the intentions-other/ half awaits a matcher/scope resolution (charter host_seam edges).
waits_on: child a waits on NOTHING (buildable now — build first). Child b (the `cairn compile` CLI face) — DONE via cairn-command. Child c (the write gate) — DONE as a FileChanged host-seam (host-noticing, an IOU standing in for the owner-gate emit state-machine-physics.json tracks; the emit lands later and retires the IOU, it no longer BLOCKS child c). SEPARATELY OWNED, not a wait here: the nightly INSPECTOR regime (held-inspectors-janitors-filters) may surface model-staleness as a compliance FINDING; its JANITOR — never this compiler — would poke the door to resolve it. Cross-referenced, owned there.
provenance: cast 2026-07-23 via /sorted, alongside cairn-command, from a single /intent that bundled a repo-scanner + an intentions-folder updater + a user-facing command runner. The name sharpened in the design conversation from `intentions-compiler` to `intentions-model-compiler` (Akien: 'right now that folder is the model', reserving `intentions-compiler` for a future prose-ingesting front-end his inner UI designer sees coming but has no intention toward yet). CORRECTION, same session: the completeness gate first flagged the scanner as a poller and I re-cast it as commit-fired — both wrong. Akien clarified the /intent's 'repo-scanner' IS the nightly INSPECTOR (held-inspectors-janitors-filters — runs once at midnight, each callback's condition is the thing inspected: code smells, misplaced files, post-build checks), a separate already-held node whose inspectors never act; the compiler's LIVE trigger is a GATE (the file-watching 'moved to gates'), not a scanner. So the bespoke scanner child was removed and the triggers corrected to CLI + /sorted + write-gate. Akien: 'sorted.'
